Eric cups his hands into a bucket of water beside a dirty sink that has long ago had its faucets removed. He drenches his face. He brushes his teeth roughly with his finger and rinses his mouth with one cupped handful from the bucket. He spits the water into the sink. He dries his face with his shirt sleeve and looks at himself in the mirror. His self-examination is strangely detached. Something broken. Something dark and hard.
